seoTitle: How to Reset TradingView Settings and Chart Style to Default
metaDescription: Cluttered charts or custom indicators taking over your view? Learn how to restore TradingView's default candlestick style, timeframes, and layout.
intro: If custom indicators, colors, or layout tweaks have left your TradingView charts looking cluttered, it's easy to reset everything back to the original default appearance. This guide covers restoring default candlestick colors and sizes, removing indicators you've added over time, and getting back to the default timeframe and layout. The same steps apply on both desktop and mobile, so it works no matter which version you use most.

steps.heading: Remove added indicators
steps.body: Clear out any custom indicators you've applied to the chart so you're starting from a clean base before resetting other visual settings.

steps.heading: Reset the candlestick style
steps.body: Restore the default candlestick colors and sizes if custom styling has changed how your chart looks compared to the original setup.

steps.heading: Restore default timeframes and layout
steps.body: Reset the chart's timeframe and overall layout back to TradingView's standard defaults.

steps.heading: Save or start a fresh layout
steps.body: Decide whether to save this reset layout for future use or start an entirely new layout from scratch, depending on your workflow.

faq.question: Can I reset TradingView settings without losing my saved layouts?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reset the active chart's style and settings while keeping other saved layouts untouched, or start a completely new layout if you prefer a clean slate.

faq.question: Does resetting TradingView settings work the same on mobile?
faq.answer: The core method for restoring default chart and candlestick style works on both the desktop and mobile versions of TradingView.

faq.question: Will resetting my chart remove indicators I paid for?
faq.answer: Resetting only removes indicators from your current chart view - it doesn't affect access to any indicators you've purchased or added to your account.

faq.question: Why would I want to reset TradingView to its default settings?
faq.answer: A reset clears away clutter from accumulated custom settings and indicators, giving you a simpler, more focused chart for analysis.
